rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative selenocyclization of N-allyl ureas with diaryl diselenides to give selenylated cyclic isoureas, with subsequent acid-catalyzed skeletal rearrangement to cyclic ureas
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of diaryl diselenide (ArSe-SeAr) to aryl selenium radical cations / ArSe+ cation; cathodic reduction of protons to H2 at platinum

key_intermediates.name: diaryl diselenide radical cation / ArSe+ cation (B -> ArSe+)
cyclic seleniranium ion (I) from ArSe+ addition to allyl alkene
kinetic 5-exo-tet cyclic isourea product C (O-cyclized, selenylated oxazoline-type)
N-protonated isourea II (after protonation of exocyclic N of C)
regenerated seleniranium I via reversible neighboring group participation (NGP) of Se

electron_flow: At the graphite anode, diaryl diselenide B is oxidized (lower onset, 1.49 V vs 1.57 V for substrate) to generate ArSe+, which electrophilically adds to the allyl alkene of urea A to give a seleniranium ion I; intramolecular 5-exo-tet attack by the urea oxygen yields cyclic isourea C, while the two released protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode. Under HClO4/heat, C is protonated at the exocyclic N (II) and undergoes reversible NGP back to I followed by irreversible N-attack to give the thermodynamic cyclic urea D.
rate_determining_step: Anodic oxidation of diaryl diselenide to generate the ArSe+ electrophile (lowest-potential, kinetically gating event)

probe_reagents.name: argon atmosphere (O2 exclusion)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction proceeds with similar yield (75%) under Ar, ruling out aerial O2 involvement

probe_reagents.name: divided cell with A1+B1 in anolyte
probe_reagents.observation: C1 formed in 55% yield, confirming oxidative (anodic) pathway

probe_reagents.name: divided cell with A1+B1 in catholyte
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed, confirming anode is the working electrode

probe_reagents.name: competition: (4-MeC6H4Se)2 (B2) vs (4-FC6H4Se)2 (B6)
probe_reagents.observation: Slight preference for electron-deficient 4-F aryl selenide product C27 (35%) over 4-Me C23 (27%), consistent with easier oxidation/transfer of more electron-poor diselenide

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of (PhSe)2 and A12
probe_reagents.observation: (PhSe)2 oxidizes at Ep=1.49 V with strong current; substrate A12 oxidizes at 1.57 V with much weaker current, indicating diselenide is preferentially oxidized

notes: Two-stage mechanism: (1) electrochemical selenocyclization gives kinetic O-cyclized isourea C; (2) separate HClO4/dioxane acid-catalyzed skeletal rearrangement converts C to thermodynamic N-cyclized urea D via reversible seleniranium reformation (NGP) then irreversible N-attack. Authors invoke H2 evolution at Pt cathode as the counter-reaction. No radical traps (TEMPO/BHT), KIE, or DFT reported; mechanism supported mainly by CV, divided-cell, and control experiments.
